vc_shape.pre_spatial: xizi_dll linklist_pre l, which unfolds to an existential last node, xizi_dllseg linklist_pre linklist_pre last l, last.node_next |-> linklist_pre, and linklist_pre.node_prev |-> last.
vc_shape.pre_pure: Zlength l <= UINT_MAX; unfolding xizi_dll also exposes linklist_pre <> NULL.
vc_shape.pre_exists: The closing node last hidden by xizi_dll.
vc_shape.post_spatial: A disjunction matching the loop invariant: for l = nil, an empty prefix plus the two sentinel self-loop fields; for l = next :: rest, an empty prefix, the head-to-next forward/backward cells, the remaining suffix segment, and the closing last/head cells.
vc_shape.post_pure: l = l1 ++ l2, Zlength l1 = 0, linklist_pre <> 0, the preserved UINT_MAX bound, and branch facts describing l2 and whether next is the sentinel.
vc_shape.post_exists: Empty branch: linklist_node_prev, tmp_node_node_next, l1, l2. Nonempty branch: last_node_next, last, next_node_prev, next, rest, l1, l2.

witnesses.witness_instantiation: Destruct l. For nil choose the left disjunct with last = linklist_pre forced by the nil segment, linklist_node_prev = tmp_node_node_next = linklist_pre, and l1 = l2 = nil. For next :: rest choose the right disjunct with l1 = nil, l2 = next :: rest, next equal to the first abstract node, and last equal to the closing node exposed by xizi_dll.
witnesses.space_plan: Unfold xizi_dll and xizi_dllseg once after destructing l. In the nil case the segment gives last = linklist_pre and emp, leaving exactly head.node_next and head.node_prev. In the cons case the segment exposes head.node_next |-> next, next.node_prev |-> head, and xizi_dllseg head next last rest; retain the closing last.node_next and head.node_prev resources and create the empty prefix by simplification.
witnesses.pure_plan: The nil segment equality rewrites the closing node to the head. In the cons case xizi_dllseg exposes next <> head; the xizi_dll definition provides head <> 0. Zlength nil = 0, nil ++ l = l, and the original bound discharge the remaining pure conjuncts.
witnesses.refinement_plan: No safeExec/refinement state occurs in this VC.
witnesses.failure_signal: None: both constructors of l are represented by the generated disjunction and all requested resources come directly from unfolding xizi_dll/xizi_dllseg.
witnesses.grouping_reason: This initializes the same bidirectional prefix/suffix invariant advanced by entail_wit_2 and closed by return_wit_1.

vc_shape.pre_spatial: A processed prefix xizi_dllseg head head tmp_node l1_2; tmp_node.node_next |-> next and next.node_prev |-> tmp_node; a suffix xizi_dllseg head next last_2 rest_2; and the closing last_2.node_next |-> head and head.node_prev |-> last_2.
vc_shape.pre_pure: next <> head; l = l1_2 ++ l2_2; l2_2 = next :: rest_2; linklist_length = Zlength l1_2; head <> 0; Zlength l <= UINT_MAX; and field-value equalities for the adjacent and closing cells.
vc_shape.pre_exists: No hidden existential before unfolding the suffix; destructing rest_2 exposes either its nil equality or its first node and tail.
vc_shape.post_spatial: The prefix extended through next as xizi_dllseg head head next l1. If rest_2 is nil, next is the last node and its next/head.prev fields close the list. If rest_2 is next_2 :: rest, unfolding the old suffix exposes next.node_next |-> next_2, next_2.node_prev |-> next, the residual suffix, and the unchanged closing fields.
vc_shape.post_pure: l = l1 ++ l2, unsigned_last_nbits (linklist_length + 1) 32 = Zlength l1, preserved head/non-null and total-length bounds, plus the branch-specific empty or cons suffix facts.
vc_shape.post_exists: Use l1 = l1_2 ++ [next]. Empty suffix branch chooses l2 = nil and last endpoint next. Nonempty suffix branch chooses l2 = next_2 :: rest, with next_2/rest obtained by destructing rest_2 and last inherited from last_2.

witnesses.witness_instantiation: Set the new processed prefix to l1_2 ++ [next]. Destruct rest_2. In the nil case xizi_dllseg head next last_2 nil forces last_2 = next, so choose the left post branch. In the cons case unfold the suffix and choose its first node and tail for next_2/rest in the right post branch.
witnesses.space_plan: Apply a group-local proved helper that appends one bidirectional edge pair to xizi_dllseg. Its caller premises are next <> head (given) and head <> next (the same fact symmetrically); separation supplies freshness against already-owned node_prev cells during the helper proof. Then destruct/unfold rest_2: nil yields endpoint equality and the closing resources; cons yields exactly the next forward/backward pair and residual segment required by the continuing branch. Frame the last/head closure throughout.
witnesses.pure_plan: Rewrite l2_2 as next :: rest_2 and l as l1_2 ++ next :: rest_2. Zlength_app and Zlength_cons show Zlength (l1_2 ++ [next]) = Zlength l1_2 + 1 and that this value is <= Zlength l. Zlength_nonneg rest_2 and Zlength l <= UINT_MAX imply 0 <= linklist_length + 1 <= UINT_MAX; with UINT_MAX = 2^32 - 1, unsigned_last_nbits_eq gives no wraparound. This is the same arithmetic route demonstrated by the permitted xizi_single_link_len reference proof.
witnesses.refinement_plan: No safeExec/refinement state occurs in this VC.

candidate_helper_declarations.statement_shape: forall head x y z l, z <> head -> x <> z -> xizi_dllseg head x y l ** y.node_next |-> z ** z.node_prev |-> y |-- xizi_dllseg head x z (l ++ [z])

premises.premise: x <> z at the call site
premises.discharged_by: x is linklist_pre and z is next, so this is exactly next <> linklist_pre by symmetry.

premise_discharge: The segment-extension helper's z <> head premise is PreH1/PreH7.
Its call-site x <> z premise is the symmetric form of PreH1 because x = head in this witness.
The unsigned no-wrap premise follows from linklist_length = Zlength l1_2, l = l1_2 ++ next :: rest_2, Zlength_nonneg rest_2, and Zlength l <= UINT_MAX.
witnesses.failure_signal: None: the only nontrivial abstraction step is a mathematically valid local segment-append lemma whose premises are all available in P; it does not require changing the seed predicate or witness statement.
witnesses.grouping_reason: This is the transition of the same prefix/suffix invariant initialized and closed by the other two witnesses; it shares predicate unfolding and list-length arithmetic with them.

vc_shape.pre_spatial: xizi_dllseg head head tmp_node l1, tmp_node.node_next |-> head, and head.node_prev |-> tmp_node.
vc_shape.pre_pure: l = l1 ++ l2; linklist_length = Zlength l1; l2 = nil; head <> 0; Zlength l <= UINT_MAX; and the closing field-value equalities.
vc_shape.post_exists: The xizi_dll closing node is instantiated with tmp_node. The theorem is a Coq-level disjunction of two identical entailments, so either side may be selected.

witnesses.witness_instantiation: Select the left side of the outer Coq disjunction. Rewrite l2 = nil and l = l1 ++ nil to l = l1; instantiate the existential last node in xizi_dll with tmp_node.
witnesses.space_plan: After rewriting l to l1, unfold xizi_dll, choose tmp_node as last, preserve head <> 0, and cancel the existing segment plus the two closing fields directly.
witnesses.pure_plan: PreH6 rewrites l2 to nil; app_nil_r in PreH2 yields l = l1; combining that with PreH3 proves linklist_length = Zlength l.
witnesses.refinement_plan: No safeExec/refinement state occurs in this VC.
witnesses.failure_signal: None: P is exactly the unfolded xizi_dll resource for last = tmp_node once l2 = nil is rewritten.
witnesses.grouping_reason: This closes the same segment invariant and reuses its predicate normalization and sequence decomposition.

analysis_groups.shared_proof_pattern: Normalize xizi_dll/xizi_dllseg, maintain the abstract decomposition l = processed_prefix ++ remaining_suffix, advance one bidirectional edge, and finally rebuild the sentinel cycle.
analysis_groups.shared_space_plan: All witnesses manipulate the same prefix segment, suffix segment, and sentinel closing pair; entail_wit_2 supplies the sole reusable segment-extension step.
analysis_groups.shared_pure_plan: List decomposition and Zlength rewriting connect initialization, increment, and return; only the transition adds unsigned no-wrap arithmetic from the global UINT_MAX bound.
candidate_helper_declarations: xizi_dllseg_extend_one (to be created with the assigned group suffix)
analysis_groups.grouping_reason: All three witnesses belong to one function and one invariant lifecycle, share the same predicate family, and total only 3 witnesses, below max_witnesses_per_group = 12. A single worker can prove them continuously and keep the one helper local to this group.

candidate_helper_declarations.statement_shape: forall head x y z l, z <> head -> x <> z -> xizi_dllseg head x y l ** y.node_next |-> z ** z.node_prev |-> y |-- xizi_dllseg head x z (l ++ [z])
premises: z <> head from the loop guard/invariant
x <> z at this call because x = head

proof_groups.proof_strategy: Prove the segment-extension helper in the group-local case_lib with suffix __dll_len_invariant. For entail_wit_1 unfold xizi_dll and destruct l to select the empty/nonempty invariant branch. For entail_wit_2 append next to the prefix with the helper, destruct rest_2 to select termination/continuation, and prove unsigned_last_nbits is exact from the total UINT_MAX bound. For return_wit_1 choose one outer disjunct, rewrite the empty suffix, and rebuild xizi_dll with last = tmp_node.

per_witness_plan.proof_of_xizi_double_link_len_entail_wit: Unfold xizi_dll; destruct l; instantiate the generated branch existentials directly from the closing-node witness and unfolded segment.
Extend l1_2 by [next], destruct rest_2, preserve/redistribute the suffix and closure resources, and establish the unsigned increment using Zlength bounds.
per_witness_plan.proof_of_xizi_double_link_len_return_wit: Choose the left duplicate disjunct, rewrite l2 = nil and l = l1, then unfold xizi_dll and instantiate last with tmp_node.

vc_shape.family: sentinel circular doubly linked list prefix/suffix invariant
vc_shape.spatial_core: xizi_dllseg prefix/suffix plus paired node_next/node_prev cells and sentinel closure
vc_shape.pure_core: sequence app decomposition, Zlength processed-prefix counter, UINT_MAX no-wrap bound

witness_instantiation.initial: empty prefix and l as remaining suffix

proof_groups.space_plan: Use predicate unfolding at the initial/terminal boundaries and one suffixed segment-extension helper at the loop transition; frame the sentinel closing cells.
proof_groups.pure_plan: Use app/Zlength normalization and the preserved total-length bound to show the C unsigned increment equals mathematical successor.

premise_discharge.segment_extension: next <> linklist_pre from PreH1/PreH7 and symmetry for linklist_pre <> next
premise_discharge.unsigned_increment: linklist_length = Zlength l1_2, l = l1_2 ++ next :: rest_2, nonnegative suffix length, and Zlength l <= UINT_MAX

proof_groups.grouping_reason: One invariant lifecycle and helper family, one function, and 3 witnesses <= controller maximum 12; no dependency or context boundary justifies splitting.

version_check.method: sha256 comparison against every source_version and source_goal_version file entry in agent_input.json

semantic_reference.use: Confirmed the analogous prefix append, Zlength_app/cons, and unsigned_last_nbits_eq no-wrap proof pattern; double-link spatial reasoning was checked against the current xizi_dllseg definition.

controller_simple_repairs.reason: vc-checking report included an inline group_plan but the default group_plan.json artifact was missing

source_goal_version.digest_input_policy: relative_path+sha256+state+role over generated files and witness statements
